title: Entrepreneurial Competence Compass
promise: Entrepreneurial competence map from opportunity recognition to responsible value creation.
audience: Entrepreneurs, intrapreneurs, students, incubators, and corporate innovation teams
description: Competence assessment inspired by EntreComp across Opportunity & Vision, Resource Mobilisation, Action & Experimentation, Ethics & Shared Value. Four-dimensional radar result, balanced index, level label, strongest-dimension archetype, weakest-dimension 30-day action, and separate answer-support checks.
ogDescription: Entrepreneurial competence map from opportunity recognition to responsible value creation.

title: Founder Readiness Radar
promise: Founder readiness diagnostic across opportunity evidence, execution, resourcefulness, economics, and resilience.

Four-dimensional radar result, balanced index, level label, strongest-dimension archetype, weakest-dimension 30-day action, and separate answer-support checks.
ogDescription: Founder readiness diagnostic across opportunity evidence, execution, resourcefulness, economics, and resilience.

ogDescription: Responsible AI and EU/Swiss readiness map for inventory, classification, data, privacy, risk, oversight, documentation, and monitoring.

promise: Sustainable value and ESG readiness diagnostic for materiality, governance, data, operations, claims, and reporting.
audience: Founders, SMEs, executive teams, and sustainability leaders
description: Sustainable value maturity assessment across Materiality & Strategy, Governance & Data, Operations & Value Chain, Claims & Reporting. Four-dimensional radar result, balanced index, level label, strongest-dimension archetype, weakest-dimension 30-day action, and separate answer-support checks.
ogTitle: Sustainable Value & ESG Readiness - Outfinity Quiz
ogDescription: Sustainable value and ESG readiness diagnostic for materiality, governance, data, operations, claims, and reporting.

title: Collaboration Species
promise: How do you actually work with others? Map your collaboration species — solo, dyad, team, weaver, or mentor — and invite colleagues for a team constellation.
description: 10 forced-choice scenarios. Mark first and second instinct (first = 3 points, second = 1). Result maps your primary and secondary collaboration species across five archetypes and suggests team compositions and the collaborators that complement you.

title: Conflict Signature
promise: What do you do when disagreement becomes real? Map your signature across five conflict approaches — confront, negotiate, accommodate, avoid, transform.
description: 8 forced-choice scenarios. Mark first and second instinct (first = 3 points, second = 1). Result maps your primary and secondary conflict approaches, where each fits, and what to watch. Challenge a friend to compare signatures.

title: Trust Architecture
promise: How do you build, verify, and lose trust? A blueprint of five trust mechanisms — relational, contractual, reputational, procedural, and communal.
description: 10 original scenarios. Each offers one option per trust mechanism. Result maps your primary and secondary mechanisms with where they fit and where they leak, with a partnership-comparison insight.

title: World Perception Challenge
promise: Is your view of the world realistic, pessimistic, or selectively distorted? 12 questions against real open data from World Bank, OWID, and WHO.
description: 12 factual estimation questions across four perception dimensions (Health, Economic, Demographic, Climate). Each question cites its source. Result maps your perception style per domain (Realistic, Selective, Pessimistic, Optimistic) and reveals where your worldview diverges from the data.

title: Work Interest Constellation
promise: What kinds of work actually energize you? A six-axis interest map inspired by Holland's RIASEC model — venture contexts, not job titles.
description: 24 original RIASEC-inspired interest items across six dimensions (Builder, Investigator, Creator, Social Guide, Persuader, Organizer). Result shows a six-axis radar with your top interests, venture suggestions, and the collaborators that complement you.

title: Builder Traits Snapshot
promise: A five-axis personality snapshot for venture builders, using public-domain IPIP items. Three minutes, no scores to maximize — only positions with a cost and a use.
description: 20 IPIP Likert items across five dimensions (Openness, Conscientiousness, Agreeableness, Emotional Stability, Achievement). Reverse-scored items prevent acquiescence bias. Result shows positions on five bipolar axes, not numeric scores.

title: Energy Economics
promise: What actually drains and recharges you? An energy ledger across six dimensions — social, novelty, structure, depth, competition, and autonomy.
description: 12 paired-activity choices (2 per dimension). In each pair, pick the activity that gives you more energy over time. Result shows a surplus/deficit ledger per dimension with poles and where to budget each.

title: Quick Big Five Mini
promise: A two-minute Big Five snapshot. 10 IPIP items, five dimensions, no score to maximize — only positions with a use.
description: 10 IPIP Likert items (2 per dimension) across Openness, Conscientiousness, Extraversion, Agreeableness, Emotional Stability. Reverse-scored items prevent acquiescence bias. Result shows brief low/moderate/high positions on five dimensions with a clear reliability disclaimer and a link to the deeper 20-item Builder Traits Snapshot.

title: Quick MBTI-Style Quiz
promise: A two-minute, MBTI-style snapshot of your four preference dimensions. Original items — not the official MBTI.
description: 12 binary-choice items across four dimensions (E/I, S/N, T/F, J/P). Custom scoring determines dominant pole per dimension and constructs a four-letter type code (e.g., INTJ) with a brief description and venture fit. Items are original and not from the official MBTI.

title: Time Horizon Portrait
promise: How do you relate to past, present, and future? Five time orientations inspired by Zimbardo Time Perspective — none prescribed as the right one.
description: 15 original Likert items across five time orientations (Past-Positive, Past-Negative, Present-Hedonistic, Present-Fatalistic, Future-Oriented). Result shows a radar of your dominant orientations and where each one costs or helps. Inspired by Zimbardo Time Perspective concepts; items are original.

title: Explorer Compass
promise: Discover your role in meaningful change, the venture ecology where your work can thrive, and who you need beside you.
description: 10 real scenarios and 8 ecology trade-offs. Your result maps your primary and secondary contribution roles, your most compatible venture ecology, and your complementarity with other explorers.

title: Social Values Map
promise: Four axes of social tension — mapped without ideological labels. Inspired by the 8values project. Tensions, not tribes.
description: 24 original 8values-inspired statements across four bipolar axes (Equality↔Merit, Local↔Global, Liberty↔Authority, Tradition↔Progress). Balanced pro/con items with reverse scoring prevent response bias. Result describes tensions, never assigns ideological labels.

title: The Basilisk Verdict
promise: A moral topology test between Moloch, Basilisk, and the Third Path. What form of power do you accept when freedom, safety, efficiency, and suffering conflict?
description: 12 dilemmas. No neutral options. Your verdict places you in one of seven archetypes on the spectrum between competitive acceleration, centralized governance, and pluralistic limits.

title: Decision Style Profile
promise: How do you decide under uncertainty? Map five axes of your decision reflex — analytics, risk, speed, control, and collaboration. No quality judgment.
description: 15 original scenario questions across five bipolar decision axes (Analytical↔Intuitive, Risk↔Caution, Speed↔Accuracy, Control↔Adaptation, Solo↔Collaborative). Each scenario offers a clear, balanced, and opposite-leaning option. Result maps positions on five axes and where your reflex fits.

title: Evidence Revision Profile
promise: How firmly do you hold beliefs — and what would change them? Four axes of the tension between conviction and openness, inspired by actively open-minded thinking.
description: 12 statements, each with a dual response: rate agreement (5-point Likert), then state what would change your mind (4 revision conditions). Custom scoring maps four axes — Conviction Strength, Revision Openness, Evidence Sensitivity, Value Anchoring — and the conviction-vs-openness tension, without judging whether you are too rigid or too flexible.

title: Intelligence Type Profile
promise: What type of intelligence do you have? A non-classical, multi-axis map of cognitive strengths — not an IQ test, no single score.
description: 20 self-rating tasks across eight intelligence types (Linguistic, Logical-Mathematical, Spatial, Musical, Bodily-Kinesthetic, Interpersonal, Intrapersonal, Naturalistic). Result shows an eight-axis radar with your top intelligences, venture contexts, and your learning style. Explicitly not an IQ test.

title: Risk Palate
promise: What risks would you actually accept? A risk menu across five life domains — financial, reputational, relational, existential, and creative.
description: 10 risk scenarios (2 per domain) with a three-option appetite scale (Never / Sometimes / Always accept). Result renders a risk menu highlighting your appetite per domain, with where it helps and where it needs a guardrail.
